title: PhyGaP: Physically-Grounded Gaussians with Polarization Cues
authors: Jiale Wu ⋅ Xiaoyang Bai ⋅ Zongqi He ⋅ Weiwei Xu ⋅ YIFAN PENG

sections.heading: TLDR / GIST
sections.gist: Recent advances in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) have demonstrated great success in modeling reflective 3D objects and their interaction with the environment via deferred rendering (DR).
sections.content: Recent advances in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) have demonstrated great success in modeling reflective 3D objects and their interaction with the environment via deferred rendering (DR). However, existing methods often struggle with correctly reconstructing physical attributes such as albedo and reflectance, and therefore they do not support high-fidelity relighting. Observing that this limitation stems from the lack of shape and material information in RGB images, we present PhyGaP, a physically-grounded 3DGS method that leverages polarization cues to facilitate precise reflection decomposition and visually consistent relighting of reconstructed objects. Specifically, we design a polarimetric deferred rendering (PolarDR) process to model polarization by reflection, and a self-occlusion-aware environment map building technique (GridMap) to resolve indirect lighting of non-convex objects. We validate on multiple synthetic and real-world scenes, including those featuring only partial polarization cues, that PhyGaP not only excels in reconstructing the appearance and surface normal of reflective 3D objects (~2 dB in PSNR and 45.7% in Cosine Distance better than existing RGB-based methods on average), but also achieves state-of-the-art inverse rendering and relighting capability.

sections.heading: Problem
sections.gist: PhyGaP addresses a key limitation in current vision systems.
sections.content: Recent advances in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) have demonstrated great success in modeling reflective 3D objects and their interaction with the environment via deferred rendering (DR). sections.heading: Key Insight
sections.gist: Observing that this limitation stems from the lack of shape and material information in RGB images, we present PhyGaP, a physically-grounded 3DGS method that leverages polarization cues to facilitate precise reflection decomposition and visually consistent relighting of reconstructed objects.
sections.content: The paper's main contribution addresses the gap identified in the problem statement, enabling improved performance on the target task.

sections.heading: Method / Approach
sections.gist: Novel approach combining advanced vision techniques with scalable training.
sections.content: Recent advances in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) have demonstrated great success in modeling reflective 3D objects and their interaction with the environment via deferred rendering (DR). However, existing methods often struggle with correctly reconstructing physical attributes such as albedo and reflectance, and therefore they do not support high-fidelity relighting.
